title: HP Can Boost SOA Offerings with Acquisition of Mercury
author: Peter S. Kastner
subject_domain: SOA management; IT governance; enterprise systems management; M&A analysis
methodology: Aberdeen Market Alert; analyst commentary on announced acquisition; primary survey data cited from prior studies
abstract: Aberdeen Market Alert analyzing HP's acquisition of Mercury Interactive, a leader in IT optimization software. HP OpenView combined with Mercury's capabilities creates a stronger SOA management, governance, performance, and development lifecycle offering. Mercury gained SOA management capabilities via acquisition of Systinet (registry) in January 2006. Aberdeen research shows 90% of companies will exit 2006 with SOA activities at least underway. Aberdeen recommends the acquisition; keys to success are integration speed, culture assimilation, and extended mainframe support. HP best suited for large enterprises; small companies (<$50M revenue) unlikely customers.
importance_rationale: Contains concrete survey data (90% SOA adoption by end 2006); analysis of major M&A with SOA implications; named-competitor market map; Kastner's role has been upgraded to VP and Research Director by July 2006.
relevance_rationale: Directly addresses SOA management and governance market circa 2006; HP-Mercury integration roadmap has concrete competitive implications. Shows mature Aberdeen SOA research program with cross-study data.
prescience_rationale: Correctly identifies SOA governance/management gap as key enterprise need; Systinet registry integration proved important; HP's subsequent history with OpenView/Mercury (eventual shift to HP Software) was more complex than predicted; mainframe concern valid but HP never fully addressed it.
